Typical Use Cases: Water level monitoring, outdoor distance measurement, tank level detection
Notes: Waterproof probe separate from controller board, range 25-450cm, works in wet environments

Typical Use Cases: Water level detection, overflow protection, liquid presence detection, tank monitoring
Notes: Non-contact detection, works through transparent containers, outputs HIGH when water detected, LOW when no water, IP65 rated probe

Typical Use Cases: Line following robots, edge detection, surface contrast detection
Notes: IR reflective sensor, detects black/white lines, outputs LOW on black line, HIGH on white surface, adjustable sensitivity via potentiometer

Typical Use Cases: Air quality monitoring, dust particle detection, PM2.5 measurement, indoor air pollution detection
Notes: Detects particles >1 micron, outputs LOW pulse proportional to dust concentration, requires 30 seconds warm-up time, 5V operation

Typical Use Cases: Oxygen concentration measurement, air quality monitoring, greenhouse monitoring, portable oxygen meter
Notes: Electrochemical sensor, measures O2 percentage (0-25%), I2C address 0x70-0x77, requires calibration in fresh air (20.9% O2), 3.3V/5V compatible

Typical Use Cases: Water quality monitoring, hydroponics, aquaponics, TDS measurement, salinity detection
Notes: Measures electrical conductivity (EC) and TDS (Total Dissolved Solids), range 0-20 mS/cm, requires temperature compensation, platinum electrode probe, analog voltage output 0-2.3

Typical Use Cases: CO2 concentration monitoring, greenhouse automation, indoor air quality, HVAC systems, incubators
Notes: Electrochemical CO2 sensor, range 0-10,000 ppm, requires 48 hours preheating for accurate readings, analog voltage output inversely proportional to CO2 (higher CO2 = lower voltage), needs calibration in fresh air (400 ppm)

Typical Use Cases: Object detection, face recognition, line tracking, color recognition, tag recognition, object classification
Notes: Built-in AI camera with LCD screen, 7 built-in AI functions, onboard training capability, no coding required for basic use, 2MP camera, recognizes faces/objects/colors/lines/tags, I2C or Serial communication

Component name: Gravity Analog Capacitive Soil Moisture Sensor
Typical Use Cases: Plant watering systems, agriculture monitoring, smart irrigation, greenhouse automation
Notes: Capacitive sensing (corrosion resistant), no exposed metal electrodes, analog voltage output (higher voltage = more moisture), range 0-100% moisture, 3.3V-5V compatible, needs calibration in dry and wet soil

Typical Use Cases: Distance measurement, obstacle detection, robot navigation, liquid level detection
Notes: UART communication (easier than trigger-echo), automatic temperature compensation, range 2-500cm, analog + serial output, built-in temperature sensor for accuracy, 3.3V-5V compatible

Typical Use Cases: Water leak detection, rain detection, liquid presence detection, flood alarm
Notes: Exposed PCB traces act as variable resistor, analog output proportional to water coverage, digital threshold output, detects water depth/coverage area, LOW cost sensor but prone to corrosion over time

Common versions: Grove Temp Sensor v1.2, NTC Thermistor
Typical Use Cases: Temperature monitoring, weather stations, environmental control, thermostat systems
Notes: NTC thermistor-based, analog output, range -40°C to +125°C, requires ADC conversion and Steinhart-Hart equation for accurate temperature calculation, 3.3V-5V compatible

Typical Use Cases: Weather stations, HVAC systems, indoor climate monitoring, agriculture, data logging
Notes: High accuracy digital sensor, I2C communication, temp range -40°C to +125°C, humidity 0-100% RH, ±0.2°C temp accuracy, ±2% RH accuracy, faster response than DHT series, I2C address 0x44 or 0x45

Typical Use Cases: Weather stations, home automation, climate monitoring, plant monitoring, basic environmental sensing
Notes: Single-wire digital protocol, temp range 0-50°C (DHT11) or -40-80°C (DHT22), humidity 20-90% RH (DHT11) or 0-100% RH (DHT22), DHT22 more accurate than DHT11, requires 1-2 second delay between readings, 3.3V-5V

Typical Use Cases: Weather stations, altitude measurement, indoor air quality, barometric pressure monitoring, drone navigation
Notes: 3-in-1 sensor, I2C or SPI communication, temp -40°C to +85°C, humidity 0-100% RH, pressure 300-1100 hPa, high accuracy (±1°C, ±3% RH, ±1 hPa), calculates altitude from pressure, I2C address 0x76 or 0x77, low power consumption

Typical Use Cases: Sound level detection, noise monitoring, voice activation, clap switches, sound-reactive projects
Notes: Microphone-based (electret mic), analog output proportional to sound intensity, does NOT record audio (only measures amplitude/volume), adjustable gain via onboard potentiometer, 3.3V-5V compatible, outputs 0-1023 range

Typical Use Cases: Motion detection, security systems, automatic lighting, occupancy detection, burglar alarms
Notes: Passive Infrared sensor, detects human/animal motion via body heat (IR radiation), digital output (HIGH on motion, LOW no motion), detection range 3-7m, 120° detection angle, requires 60 sec warm-up time, adjustable sensitivity and delay time via potentiometers

Typical Use Cases: Soil moisture detection, plant watering systems, garden automation, humidity sensing
Notes: Resistive sensor with exposed metal probes, analog output inversely proportional to moisture (dry = high value, wet = low value), simple and low-cost but probes corrode over time, not suitable for long-term soil deployment, 3.3V-5V compatible

Typical Use Cases: Ambient light detection, automatic lighting control, day/night detection, light level monitoring
Notes: Photoresistor (LDR) based, analog output proportional to light intensity (higher light = higher voltage), resistance decreases with increased light, simple voltage divider circuit, 3.3V-5V compatible, wide detection range from darkness to bright sunlight

Common versions: Grove IR Distance Interrupter v1.2, RPI-352
Typical Use Cases: Object detection, proximity sensing, obstacle detection, speed measurement, rotation counting, encoder
Notes: Infrared transmitter-receiver pair (photointerrupter), digital output (LOW when object detected/beam blocked, HIGH when clear), detection distance 1-8cm adjustable via potentiometer, narrow slot design for precise detection, 3.3V-5V compatible

Common versions: Grove IR Reflective Sensor v1.2, RPR-220
Typical Use Cases: Line following robots, proximity detection, surface color detection, edge detection, object detection
Notes: IR LED + phototransistor pair, detects reflected IR light, digital output (LOW when object/white surface detected, HIGH when no reflection/black surface), detection distance 4-15mm, adjustable sensitivity via potentiometer, 3.3V-5V compatible

Common versions: Grove Hall Sensor v1.2, A1324, OH137
Typical Use Cases: Magnetic field detection, proximity sensing, speed measurement, door/window sensors, brushless motor position, rotation counting
Notes: Detects presence of magnetic field (magnets), digital output (LOW when magnet near south pole detected, HIGH when no magnet or north pole), detection distance 1-3cm, non-contact sensing, works through non-magnetic materials, 3.3V-5V compatible

Typical Use Cases: UV index measurement, sunlight intensity monitoring, solar panel optimization, skin protection alerts, weather stations
Notes: Measures visible light, UV index, and infrared light separately, I2C communication, SI1145 digital sensor chip, outputs lux for visible light and UV index value, I2C address 0x60, ideal for outdoor/solar projects, 3.3V-5V compatible

Typical Use Cases: CO and combustible gas detection, gas leak detection, safety monitoring, air quality sensing
Notes: Detects carbon monoxide (CO) and flammable gases (LPG, methane, propane), analog output proportional to gas concentration, requires 24-48 hours preheating for stable readings, heating element inside, sensitive to multiple gases, 5V power required, analog voltage increases with gas concentration

Typical Use Cases: Barometric pressure measurement, altitude calculation, weather forecasting, drone navigation, indoor positioning
Notes: 2-in-1 sensor (pressure + temperature), I2C or SPI communication, pressure range 300-1100 hPa, temp -40°C to +85°C, calculates altitude from pressure, high accuracy (±1 hPa, ±1°C), I2C address 0x76 or 0x77, low power consumption, successor to BMP180

Common versions: Grove Air Quality Sensor v1.3, MP503
Typical Use Cases: Indoor air quality monitoring, harmful gas detection, air pollution detection, ventilation control, smart home air quality
Notes: Detects multiple harmful gases (CO, alcohol, acetone, formaldehyde, etc.), analog output with quality levels (1=fresh, 2=low pollution, 3=high pollution, 4=very high pollution), requires 2-3 minutes warm-up, MP503 sensor chip, heating element inside, 5V power required

Common versions: Grove 80cm IR Proximity Sensor, Sharp GP2Y0A21YK
Typical Use Cases: Distance measurement, obstacle detection, proximity sensing, robot navigation, object detection
Notes: Sharp IR distance sensor, analog output inversely proportional to distance, detection range 10-80cm, non-contact measurement, immune to ambient light interference, faster response than ultrasonic, analog voltage decreases as distance increases, 3.3V-5V compatible

Typical Use Cases: Motion tracking, orientation detection, gesture recognition, robotics, drones, fall detection, step counting
Notes: 6-DOF IMU (3-axis accelerometer + 3-axis gyroscope), I2C communication, measures acceleration (-2g to +16g) and angular velocity (-250 to +2000 °/s), built-in DMP (Digital Motion Processor) for orientation calculation, I2C address 0x68 or 0x69, 3.3V-5V compatible

Common versions: Grove IR Receiver v1.2, VS1838B, TSOP382
Typical Use Cases: Remote control reception, wireless communication, IR remote projects, appliance control
Notes: Receives IR signals from remote controls, decodes IR protocols (NEC, Sony, RC5, etc.), digital output with encoded data, 38kHz carrier frequency, detection range 8-10m, paired with IR transmitter for wireless control, 3.3V-5V compatible

Typical Use Cases: Ozone concentration measurement, air quality monitoring, disinfection systems, environmental monitoring
Notes: Electrochemical ozone sensor, I2C communication, measures O3 concentration 0-10 ppm, high sensitivity and selectivity for ozone, requires calibration, I2C address 0x70-0x77, 3.3V-5V compatible, suitable for indoor/outdoor air quality monitoring

Typical Use Cases: Gesture recognition, proximity detection, RGB color sensing, ambient light sensing, touchless control
Notes: 4-in-1 sensor (gesture + proximity + RGB color + ambient light), I2C communication, detects up/down/left/right/near/far gestures, proximity range 0-10cm, UV/IR blocking filter, I2C address 0x39, 3.3V-5V compatible, enables touchless interfaces

Typical Use Cases: Access control, authentication, contactless payment, ID card reading, smart locks, attendance systems
Notes: Reads/writes NFC/RFID tags and cards, supports multiple protocols (ISO14443A/B, Mifare, FeliCa), communication via I2C/SPI/UART, detection range 4-10cm, includes 2 transponder tags/cards (13.56MHz), can read UID and store data on tags, 3.3V-5V compatible

Typical Use Cases: Motion detection, presence sensing, security systems, automatic lighting, object detection through walls
Notes: Microwave doppler radar, detects moving objects through walls/glass/plastic, digital output (HIGH on motion, LOW when static), detection range 5-9m, 360° detection through non-metallic materials, works in darkness, more sensitive than PIR, detects micro-movements, 4-28V power (typically 5V)

Typical Use Cases: Motion detection, tilt sensing, vibration monitoring, orientation detection, tap detection, fall detection, step counting
Notes: 3-axis MEMS accelerometer, I2C or SPI communication, measures acceleration on X/Y/Z axes, range ±2g to ±16g (selectable), detects tilt/orientation/motion/vibration/shock, built-in tap and free-fall detection, I2C address 0x53 (ADXL345) or 0x18/0x19 (LIS3DH), 3.3V-5V compatible

Typical Use Cases: Water quality monitoring, aquarium/aquaponics pH control, hydroponics, pool monitoring, laboratory pH measurement
Notes: Glass electrode pH probe, analog voltage output, measures pH 0-14, requires temperature compensation for accuracy, BNC connector for probe, includes calibration solutions (pH 4.0, 7.0), signal conversion board + probe kit, 3.3V-5V compatible, requires 2-point or 3-point calibration

Typical Use Cases: Color recognition, sorting, matching, RGB value measurement, light color temperature
Notes: 4-channel RGBC (Red, Green, Blue, Clear) sensor, I2C communication, 16-bit resolution per channel, built-in IR blocking filter, adjustable integration time and gain, I2C address 0x29, 3.3V-5V compatible, includes white LED for consistent lighting

Typical Use Cases: Heart rate monitoring, fitness tracking, health monitoring, pulse detection
Notes: Photoplethysmography (PPG) sensor, detects heart rate via light reflection from blood flow, analog or I2C output depending on version, finger or earlobe placement, range 30-240 BPM, requires contact with skin, 3.3V-5V compatible

Typical Use Cases: Power consumption monitoring, battery monitoring, solar panel efficiency, current/voltage/power measurement
Notes: Measures voltage (0-26V), current (0-3.2A max), and calculates power, I2C communication, I2C address 0x40-0x4F (configurable), 12-bit ADC resolution, 3.3V-5V compatible, useful for energy monitoring projects

Typical Use Cases: Water leak detection, liquid level detection, conductive liquid presence
Notes: Digital switch based on conductivity, outputs HIGH when conductive liquid detected, LOW when dry, simple 2-probe design, 3.3V-5V compatible, suitable for water/saline solution detection

Typical Use Cases: Vibration detection, shock detection, earthquake sensing, anti-theft alarms
Notes: Spring-based vibration switch, digital output (HIGH when shaken, LOW when still), adjustable sensitivity via potentiometer, detects physical shock/vibration, 3.3V-5V compatible, simple mechanical switch

Typical Use Cases: Line following (grayscale), surface reflectivity measurement, brightness detection
Notes: IR reflective sensor with analog output, measures grayscale levels (not just black/white), analog voltage proportional to surface brightness, detection distance 2-10mm, 3.3V-5V compatible, more precise than digital line sensors

Typical Use Cases: Temperature monitoring, thermostat control, environmental sensing
Notes: Precision analog temperature sensor, outputs 10mV per °C, range 0-100°C (LM35) or -55-150°C (LM35A), linear analog output, no calibration needed, simple voltage-to-temperature conversion, 4-30V power, very accurate (±0.5°C)

Common versions: DFRobot Ambient Light, BH1750 analog version
Typical Use Cases: Ambient light measurement, auto-brightness control, day/night detection
Notes: Analog output proportional to light intensity (lux), wide range detection, simple photoresistor or photodiode based, analog voltage increases with light level, 3.3V-5V compatible, similar to Grove Light Sensor but Gravity connector

Typical Use Cases: Vibration detection, shock alarm, knock detection, anti-theft systems
Notes: Spring-based vibration switch, digital output (HIGH when vibration detected, LOW when still), detects physical shock/knock/vibration, adjustable sensitivity on some versions, 3.3V-5V compatible, similar to shake sensor but more sensitive

Typical Use Cases: Tilt detection, orientation sensing, angle detection, position monitoring

Typical Use Cases: Touch input, capacitive buttons, touchless control, interactive projects
Notes: Capacitive touch detection, digital output (HIGH when touched, LOW when not), no mechanical parts (solid-state), works through thin plastic/glass, adjustable sensitivity, toggle or momentary mode selectable, 3.3V-5V compatible

Typical Use Cases: Carbon monoxide detection, gas leak alarm, air quality monitoring, safety systems
Notes: Semiconductor gas sensor, detects CO (10-500 ppm), analog output proportional to CO concentration, requires 24-48 hours preheating, heating element inside (gets warm), 5V power required, sensitive to CO specifically, ~150-180mA current draw

Typical Use Cases: High voltage measurement, battery monitoring, voltage sensing (up to 25V)
Notes: Steps down 0-25V to 0-5V for Arduino ADC, resistor divider circuit, analog output, allows Arduino to measure voltages higher than 5V safely, 1/5 voltage division ratio, no library needed, simple analog read with conversion

Component name: Gravity Digital Piezo Disk Vibration Sensor

Typical Use Cases: Game controllers, robot control, 2D position input, menu navigation

Typical Use Cases: User input, control switches, menu navigation, triggers

Typical Use Cases: Emergency stops, user input, control switches, alarms

Typical Use Cases: User input, control switches, menu navigation, warnings
Notes: Momentary pushbutton (normally open), digital output (LOW when pressed, HIGH when released with pull-up), yellow cap for caution/warning functions, tactile feedback, debouncing recommended, 3.3V-5V compatible

Notes: High-brightness white LED module, digital control (HIGH = on, LOW = off), current-limiting resistor included, 3.3V-5V compatible, simple on/off control

Typical Use Cases: Sound output, alarms, music, notifications, audio feedback

Component name: Gravity I2C 16x2 LCD with RGB Backlight

Notes: 16 columns × 2 rows character display, I2C communication (only 2 wires), RGB backlight (adjustable colors), I2C address typically 0x3E or 0x7C, displays ASCII characters, 5V power, much easier wiring than parallel LCD

Typical Use Cases: Touch input, capacitive buttons, interactive projects, touch-activated controls
Notes: Capacitive touch sensor, digital output (HIGH when touched, LOW when not touched), detects touch through thin materials (plastic, glass), toggle mode or momentary mode selectable via jumper, no mechanical parts (solid-state), 3.3V-5V compatible, single touch point

Common versions: AM2302, DHT22 Pro, Grove DHT Pro

Typical Use Cases: Alarms, beeps, notifications, sound alerts
Notes: Active buzzer (built-in oscillator), produces fixed tone (2.5 kHz) when powered, no PWM required, simple on/off control, 3.3V-5V compatible, louder than passive buzzers

Typical Use Cases: Voice activation, sound detection, clap switches, audio triggers

Typical Use Cases: Heart rate monitoring, pulse detection, fitness projects
Notes: Photoresistor-based pulse sensor, finger placement required, analog output fluctuates with heartbeat, NOT for medical use, simple pulse detection via LED and photoresistor, 3.3V-5V compatible

Typical Use Cases: High voltage control, AC appliance control, lamp switching, home automation
Notes: 5V single-channel relay, controls high voltage/current devices (AC 250V or DC 30V), SPDT configuration, digital control (HIGH = on, LOW = off), optical isolation, audible click when switching

Typical Use Cases: IR remote control, wireless communication, TV control, home automation
Notes: IR LED transmitter, sends IR signals/codes, 38kHz carrier frequency, paired with IR receiver for wireless control, range 5-10m, 3.3V-5V compatible, use IRremote library to send codes

Typical Use Cases: Knock detection, door triggers, tap sensing, vibration alarms
Notes: Detects knocking/tapping vibrations, digital output (HIGH on knock), adjustable sensitivity, spring-based sensor, 3.3V-5V compatible, great for "secret knock" door locks

Typical Use Cases: Object detection, counting, beam-break detection, card readers
Notes: Transmitter-receiver pair, digital output when beam broken, detects objects passing between sensors, narrow detection gap, 3.3V-5V compatible, similar to IR distance interrupter

Typical Use Cases: Door/window sensors, proximity detection, magnetic triggers
Notes: Reed switch activated by magnetic field, digital output changes when magnet nearby, simple mechanical switch (glass tube with contacts), detection distance 1-2cm, 3.3V-5V compatible

Typical Use Cases: Touch detection, metal touch sensing, touch lamps, interactive art
Notes: Detects human touch on metal surfaces, both analog and digital outputs, touch sensitivity adjustable, works through direct metal contact, 3.3V-5V compatible

Typical Use Cases: Robot obstacle avoidance, proximity detection, collision prevention
Notes: IR reflective sensor, detects objects in front, digital output (LOW when obstacle detected), adjustable detection distance via potentiometer (2-30cm typical), 3.3V-5V compatible

Typical Use Cases: Menu navigation, volume control, position sensing, stepper motor control
Notes: Incremental rotary encoder with push button, outputs pulses on rotation (CLK, DT pins), detects rotation direction, push button switch included, 3.3V-5V compatible, requires interrupt or polling

Typical Use Cases: Tilt detection, orientation sensing, anti-theft alarms
Notes: Mercury-free ball tilt switch, digital output changes when tilted, simple mechanical switch (rolling ball), detects tilt angle ~30-60°, 3.3V-5V compatible

Typical Use Cases: Line following robots, path tracking, line detection
Notes: IR reflective sensor for line following, digital output (LOW on black line, HIGH on white), adjustable sensitivity, detects black/white contrast, 3.3V-5V compatible, similar to Grove Line Finder

Typical Use Cases: Status indicators, go/stop signals, dual-state display
Notes: Bi-color LED (red/green), common cathode, 2 control pins (red, green), can display red, green, or yellow (both on), 3.3V-5V compatible

Typical Use Cases: Movement detection, shake detection, anti-theft alarms
Notes: Spring-based vibration switch, digital output (HIGH when vibration detected), detects physical movement/shaking, simple mechanical switch, 3.3V-5V compatible

Typical Use Cases: General purpose projects, beginner projects, prototyping
Notes: ATmega328P (R3) or Renesas RA4M1 (R4), 5V logic, 14 digital pins (6 PWM), 6 analog pins, 32KB flash, USB connection, most common Arduino board

Common versions: Arduino Nano, Nano Every, Nano 33 IoT
Typical Use Cases: Compact projects, breadboard-friendly, space-constrained designs
Notes: ATmega328P (classic) or ATmega4809 (Every), 5V logic (classic/Every) or 3.3V (33 IoT), 14 digital pins (6 PWM), 8 analog pins, smaller footprint than Uno, breadboard compatible

Typical Use Cases: Complex projects with many sensors, robotics, 3D printers
Notes: ATmega2560, 5V logic, 54 digital pins (15 PWM), 16 analog pins, 256KB flash, 4 hardware serial ports, ideal for multi-sensor projects

Typical Use Cases: USB HID projects, keyboard/mouse emulation, USB devices
Notes: ATmega32u4, 5V logic, 20 digital pins (7 PWM), 12 analog pins, native USB support (can act as keyboard/mouse), single hardware serial port

Typical Use Cases: High-performance projects, fast data processing, audio/video projects
Notes: ARM Cortex-M3 (SAM3X8E), 3.3V logic (NOT 5V tolerant!), 54 digital pins (12 PWM), 12 analog inputs (12-bit), 84 MHz, 512KB flash, 2 DAC outputs

Typical Use Cases: IoT projects, WiFi/Bluetooth communication, web servers, smart home
Notes: Tensilica Xtensa dual-core, 3.3V logic, 34 digital pins, 18 analog pins (12-bit ADC), built-in WiFi + Bluetooth, 240 MHz, 520KB SRAM, ideal for wireless projects

Typical Use Cases: WiFi IoT projects, web servers, cloud connectivity, low-cost wireless
Notes: Tensilica L106 32-bit, 3.3V logic, limited GPIO pins (depends on module), 1 analog pin (10-bit ADC), built-in WiFi (no Bluetooth), 80/160 MHz, lower cost than ESP32

Typical Use Cases: Compact USB HID projects, wearables, small robotics
Notes: ATmega32u4, 5V logic, 20 digital pins (7 PWM), 12 analog pins, native USB (keyboard/mouse emulation), very compact, breadboard compatible

Typical Use Cases: Low-power projects, battery-powered devices, embedded systems
Notes: ATmega328P, 5V or 3.3V versions available, 14 digital pins (6 PWM), 6 analog pins, no USB (requires FTDI adapter), very low power consumption, smallest Arduino form factor

Typical Use Cases: WiFi IoT projects, cloud connectivity, compact wireless projects
Notes: ARM Cortex-M0+ (SAMD21), 3.3V logic, 14 digital pins, 8 analog pins, built-in WiFi + Bluetooth, IMU sensor included, secure element for encryption, compact size

Typical Use Cases: Prototyping, temporary circuits, testing, learning projects
Notes: Solderless prototyping board, allows temporary component connections, power rails (+/-), tie points (rows/columns), common sizes: full (830), half (400), mini (170), reusable, no code generation but essential for physical setup

Typical Use Cases: Connecting components, breadboard wiring, prototyping
Notes: Pre-cut insulated wires with connectors, color-coded, various lengths (10-30cm common), M-M for breadboard, M-F for Arduino to breadboard, F-F for sensor modules, highly visible in project images

Common versions: 9V battery, AA battery pack, USB power, wall adapter
Typical Use Cases: Powering Arduino projects, portable power, standalone operation
Notes: External power sources, 9V battery (with barrel jack), AA battery holders (4x1.5V = 6V), USB power banks, wall adapters (7-12V), visible in project images when used

Typical Use Cases: Current limiting (LEDs), pull-up/pull-down, voltage dividers
Notes: Passive component, limits current flow, color-coded bands indicate value, essential for LEDs (220Ω-1kΩ), buttons (10kΩ pull-up/down), commonly visible in breadboard projects, no code but affects circuit function

Typical Use Cases: On/Off control, mode selection, power switching, device control
Notes: Mechanical switch with lever/toggle, maintains position (not momentary), SPST (single pole single throw - on/off), SPDT (single pole double throw - 3 positions), DPDT (double pole), commonly visible in projects, 2-3 pins, manual flip required

Typical Use Cases: Power on/off, mode selection, compact switching, device enable/disable
Notes: Sliding mechanism switch, SPDT most common, maintains position, compact size, 3 pins (common + 2 positions), often used for power control or mode switching, slide left/right operation

Typical Use Cases: Power switching, panel mounting, on/off control, appliance control
Notes: Panel-mount rocking switch, larger than toggle, maintains position, often illuminated versions available, rectangular housing, used for main power control, professional appearance

Common versions: USB Type-A to Type-B, USB Type-A to Micro-USB, USB Type-A to USB-C
Typical Use Cases: Programming Arduino, serial communication, power supply, data transfer
Notes: Connects Arduino to computer, used for uploading code and serial monitor, provides 5V power up to 500mA, Type-B (Uno/Mega), Micro-USB (Nano/ESP32/Leonardo), USB-C (newer boards like Uno R4), typically 1-3m length

Typical Use Cases: Graphics display, data visualization, menu systems, status screens, gauges
Function Names: begin(), display(), clearDisplay(), drawPixel(), print(), drawLine(), drawRect()
Notes: Organic LED display, I2C (2 wires) or SPI (4+ wires), high contrast no backlight needed, 128x64 or 128x32 monochrome most common, 0.96" or 1.3" sizes, lower power than LCD, sharper text, I2C address 0x3C or 0x3D, white/blue/yellow color options

Typical Use Cases: Color graphics, touch interfaces, image display, games, dashboards
Function Names: begin(), fillScreen(), drawRect(), drawCircle(), drawBitmap(), setTextColor()
Notes: Thin Film Transistor LCD, full color 16-bit (65K colors), SPI interface, resistive/capacitive touchscreen versions available, common sizes 1.8"-3.5", higher power than OLED, requires backlight, used for colorful UIs and graphics, 240x320 or 128x160 resolution typical

Common versions: Single digit, 4-digit with TM1637, 8-digit with MAX7219
Typical Use Cases: Number display, clocks, counters, temperature display, timers, score boards
Notes: LED segments forming numbers 0-9 and some letters, single or multi-digit modules, common anode or common cathode types, TM1637 (I2C-like 2-wire, 4-digit common), MAX7219 (SPI, 8-digit), bright red/green/blue LEDs, excellent for counters/clocks/numeric data, decimal point included

Typical Use Cases: Animations, scrolling text, graphics, games, visual effects, pixel art
Notes: Grid of LEDs (8x8, 16x16, 32x32 common), MAX7219 driver chip for monochrome red matrices, WS2812B for RGB addressable, can chain multiple matrices together, displays patterns/text/animations/games, monochrome or full RGB versions, 5V power, great for retro displays

Typical Use Cases: Low-power display, outdoor visibility, battery projects, retro projects, portable devices
Function Names: begin(), display(), clearDisplay(), drawPixel(), print(), drawLine()
Notes: 84x48 pixel monochrome LCD, extremely low power consumption, highly visible in direct sunlight, SPI interface (5 pins), salvaged from Nokia 5110 phones, good for battery-powered projects, blue backlight option, retro aesthetic, cheaper than OLED

Typical Use Cases: Musical tones, melodies, variable frequency sounds, alarms
Notes: Passive buzzer requires PWM signal, can produce different frequencies/notes, tone() function to play specific frequencies, no built-in oscillator (unlike active buzzer), 3.3V-5V compatible, great for playing simple melodies

Typical Use Cases: Playing audio files, voice feedback, music playback, sound effects
Notes: Plays MP3/WAV files from SD card or onboard flash, serial communication (UART), built-in amplifier (can drive 3W speaker), volume control, folder/track selection, 3.3V-5V compatible, great for voice prompts and music

Typical Use Cases: Amplifying audio signals, driving speakers, sound output
Notes: Audio amplifier, boosts weak audio signals to drive speakers, PAM8403 (3W stereo), LM386 (low power), TDA2030 (high power), requires audio input signal, 5V-12V power, potentiometer for volume control

Typical Use Cases: RGB lighting effects, ambient lighting, decorative lighting, animations
Notes: Addressable RGB LED strip, each LED individually controllable, 5V power, single data pin control, can chain multiple LEDs (30/60/144 LEDs per meter), creates rainbow/chase/fade effects, 3 wires (5V, GND, Data), high current draw

Typical Use Cases: Laser pointers, light shows, alignment tools, distance measurement triggers
Notes: Laser diode module (typically red 650nm), 5mW safe class, digital on/off control, dot projection, WARNING: never point at eyes, often includes focusing lens, 3.3V-5V compatible, used with LDR for laser trip-wire systems

Typical Use Cases: Basic indicators, status lights, simple lighting, flashlights
Notes: Standard single-color LED, 5mm or 3mm size, requires current-limiting resistor (220Ω-1kΩ), forward voltage 1.8V-3.3V depending on color, digital on/off control, various colors available, anode (+) longer leg, 3.3V-5V compatible

Typical Use Cases: Currency verification, UV curing, fluorescence detection, sterilization
Notes: Ultraviolet LED (typically 395-405nm near-UV), makes fluorescent materials glow, requires current-limiting resistor, 3.2-3.6V forward voltage, digital on/off control, used for detecting UV-reactive inks, 3.3V-5V compatible with resistor

Typical Use Cases: Precise angle control, robotic arms, steering, camera gimbals, pan-tilt
Notes: Position-controlled motor, 0-180° rotation (standard) or 0-360° (continuous), PWM control (50Hz, 1-2ms pulse), 3 wires (Power, GND, Signal), SG90 (small/hobby), MG995 (metal gear/stronger), requires external 5V-6V power for multiple servos, torque rated in kg·cm

Typical Use Cases: Precise positioning, CNC machines, 3D printers, robotics, camera sliders
Notes: Precise step-by-step rotation, 200 steps/rev (1.8°) typical for NEMA, 2048 steps/rev for 28BYJ-48 with gearbox, requires driver (ULN2003, A4988, DRV8825), unipolar or bipolar types, holds position when powered, no encoder needed, high torque at low speed

Typical Use Cases: Robot wheels, fans, drills, continuous rotation, speed control
Notes: DC motor with H-bridge driver module, controls speed (PWM) and direction (2 digital pins), L298N drives 2 motors up to 2A each, allows forward/reverse/brake, external power supply 5V-35V, heat sink included, enables motor control from Arduino's low-current pins

Typical Use Cases: Drones, quadcopters, high-speed applications, cooling fans
Notes: Brushless motor requires ESC (Electronic Speed Controller), 3 wires to ESC, ESC controlled like servo (PWM 1-2ms), high RPM (KV rating = RPM per volt), very efficient, no brushes (longer life), used in drones/RC planes, requires LiPo battery (11.1V-14.8V typical)

Typical Use Cases: High-torque low-speed applications, robot wheels, conveyor belts
Notes: DC motor with integrated gearbox, reduces speed and increases torque, various gear ratios (1:10 to 1:1000), typically 3V-12V, requires motor driver (L298N, TB6612), yellow TT motors common in robot kits, encoder versions available for position feedback

Typical Use Cases: Electric locks, valves, mechanical actuators, door latches
Notes: Electromagnetic coil creates linear motion, push or pull type, requires transistor/MOSFET/relay driver (high current), 5V-24V power (separate from Arduino), generates magnetic field to move plunger, typically 12V 0.5A-2A, used for locking mechanisms and valve control

Typical Use Cases: AC device control, heaters, high-power switching, industrial control
Notes: No mechanical parts (semiconductor switching), silent operation, faster switching than mechanical relay, controls AC loads (25A-100A typical), requires heat sink for high current, optical isolation, 3-32V DC control input, switches AC 24-380V, longer lifespan than mechanical relays

Typical Use Cases: Cooling/heating, temperature control, mini-fridges, beverage coolers
Notes: Thermoelectric cooler/heater, creates temperature difference when powered, one side cools while other heats, reversible (swap polarity), 12V typical (3-6A), requires heat sink and fan, PWM for temperature regulation, used with temperature sensor for control loop, max temp difference ~65°C

Common versions: Silicone Heat Pad, Cartridge Heater, PTC Heater
Typical Use Cases: 3D printer heated bed, incubation, temperature regulation
Notes: Resistive heating element, converts electricity to heat, requires high current (cannot drive directly from Arduino), use relay or SSR for control, 12V-24V typical, various forms (pad/cartridge/wire), use with temperature sensor and PID control for precise temperature, safety: thermal fuse recommended

Typical Use Cases: Water flow control, irrigation, pneumatic systems, gas flow
Notes: Electrically controlled valve, opens/closes fluid/gas flow, 2-way (on/off) or 3-way (diverter), requires relay or MOSFET driver, 12V-24V typical, normally closed (NC) or normally open (NO), use in irrigation systems, washing machines, pneumatic automation

Typical Use Cases: Advanced haptic feedback, wearables, tactile notifications, VR controllers
Notes: Advanced haptic actuator, smoother vibration than ERM motor, uses DRV2605 driver chip, I2C control, 123 pre-programmed effects (clicks, buzzes, pulses), realistic tactile feedback, lower power than vibration motor, used in smartphones/wearables, 3.3V-5V compatible

Typical Use Cases: Custom ultrasonic projects, air flow detection, object detection
Notes: Separate ultrasonic transmitter (TX) and receiver (RX) modules, 40kHz frequency, used to build custom ultrasonic systems, unlike HC-SR04 which has integrated pair, requires driver circuit, used in robotics, parking sensors, fluid level detection

Typical Use Cases: Weight measurement, scales, force sensing, pressure detection
Notes: Converts force/weight to electrical signal, requires HX711 ADC amplifier module, strain gauge based, various capacities (1kg-200kg), very precise (0.1g resolution possible), 4-wire Wheatstone bridge, requires calibration with known weights, used in digital scales, force plates

Typical Use Cases: Gesture control, finger motion, robotics, posture detection
Notes: Resistive sensor that changes resistance when bent, analog output (0-1023), typically 10kΩ-100kΩ resistance range, one-axis bending detection, used in gloves for gesture recognition, prosthetics, requires voltage divider circuit, measures bend angle 0-90°, single-use direction (bends one way)

Typical Use Cases: Touch pressure detection, weight sensing, force feedback, pressure pads
Notes: Changes resistance based on applied pressure/force, analog output, not for precise weight measurement (use load cell instead), detects presence and relative force, circular or rectangular pads, requires voltage divider (10kΩ resistor), used in electronic drums, touch interfaces, robotics grippers, responds to 0.1N-10N force typical

Typical Use Cases: Knock detection, vibration sensing, impact sensing, drum triggers
Notes: Generates voltage when mechanically stressed (piezoelectric effect), analog voltage output (can be several volts), detects taps/knocks/vibrations, bidirectional (can also be used as buzzer), requires high-impedance input or voltage divider, used for contact microphones, earthquake detection, drum pads

Typical Use Cases: Specific gas detection, air quality, industrial monitoring
Notes: Converts chemical concentration to electrical signal, highly selective (specific gases), examples already in your list: O2 (ID 5), CO2 (ID 7), Ozone (ID 30), pH (ID 36-37), requires warm-up time, limited lifespan (1-3 years), this is a category entry to cover other electrochemical sensors not already listed

Typical Use Cases: High temperature measurement, ovens, kilns, engine monitoring
Notes: Junction of two dissimilar metals creates voltage (Seebeck effect), measures very high temperatures (-200°C to +1350°C for K-type), requires cold junction compensation (MAX31855 chip), SPI communication, more accurate than thermistor at high temps, various types (K, J, T, etc.), used in 3D printers, reflow ovens, industrial

Typical Use Cases: Audio recording, voice recognition, sound analysis, FFT
Notes: Micro-Electro-Mechanical Systems microphone, better quality than electret mics, I2S digital output (for ESP32/advanced) or analog output, flat frequency response, low noise, omnidirectional, used for voice assistants, audio recording, FFT spectrum analysis, 3.3V power, small SMD package

Typical Use Cases: Wireless serial communication, remote control, data transfer, smartphone control
Notes: Bluetooth 2.0 SPP (Serial Port Profile), both master and slave mode (HC-05), transmit AND receive, serial communication at 9600 baud default, 10m range, 3.3V logic (use voltage divider for 5V Arduino TX), pairs with smartphones/computers, AT commands for configuration, HC-06 is receive-only (slave mode only)

Typical Use Cases: Low-power wireless communication, wearables, beacon projects, smartphone apps
Notes: Bluetooth 4.0 Low Energy (BLE), much lower power than HC-05, transmit AND receive, serial communication, 3.3V logic level, works with modern smartphones (iOS/Android), 100m range (open space), iBeacon compatible, AT commands for configuration, used in fitness trackers, IoT sensors

Common versions: NRF24L01, NRF24L01+ (with PA+LNA for longer range)
Typical Use Cases: Wireless sensor networks, remote control, multi-node communication, drones
Notes: 2.4GHz radio transceiver, bidirectional communication (TX/RX), SPI interface, 100m range (standard) or 1km+ (PA+LNA version), 6 data pipes for multi-device networks, low power consumption, 250kbps-2Mbps data rate, requires 3.3V power (sensitive to voltage), used for wireless sensor networks, RC projects

Typical Use Cases: Long-range wireless communication, telemetry, remote monitoring, outdoor projects
Notes: 433MHz or 470MHz radio transceiver, bidirectional serial communication, 1.8km range (open space with antenna), serial interface (like wireless UART), 9600 baud default, multiple power modes (low power available), AT commands for configuration, outdoor line-of-sight works best, antenna required for best range

Typical Use Cases: Long-range IoT, remote sensors, smart agriculture, city-wide networks
Notes: Long Range (LoRa) transceiver, 2-15km range depending on environment, 433MHz or 868/915MHz bands, very low power, bidirectional communication, SPI interface, slow data rate (300bps-50kbps trade-off for range), used in smart cities, agriculture, wildlife tracking, LoRaWAN protocol for networks

Common versions: XBee Series 1, XBee Series 2, XBee Pro
Typical Use Cases: Mesh networks, home automation, sensor networks, industrial control
Notes: ZigBee protocol transceiver, mesh networking (nodes relay messages), 100m-1.6km range (Pro version), low power, serial interface, 2.4GHz frequency, point-to-point or mesh topology, coordinator/router/end-device roles, more expensive but professional-grade, used in smart homes, industrial automation

Typical Use Cases: Peer-to-peer communication, sensor networks, fast data transfer without WiFi router
Notes: Connectionless communication protocol by Espressif, works without WiFi router, direct ESP-to-ESP communication, 250m range, very fast connection (<20ms), 250 bytes per packet, supports broadcast and unicast, up to 20 paired devices, lower power than WiFi, built into ESP32/ESP8266, great for sensor networks

Typical Use Cases: SMS/calls, cellular data, GPS tracking, remote monitoring
Notes: GSM/GPRS transceiver, requires SIM card, send/receive SMS and voice calls, GPRS data (2G speeds), serial interface (UART), AT commands, 4.2V power (boost converter needed from 5V), antenna required, used for SMS alerts, vehicle tracking, remote control anywhere with cell coverage

Typical Use Cases: IoT cellular connectivity, asset tracking, remote monitoring, smart meters
Notes: LTE CAT-M1 and NB-IoT transceiver, modern cellular IoT protocol, lower power than GSM, better coverage than GSM in buildings, requires compatible SIM card, GPS built-in, serial interface, used for battery-powered remote sensors, smart city applications, agricultural monitoring

Typical Use Cases: Location tracking, navigation, timestamp, geo-fencing, drone positioning
Notes: GPS receiver (receives satellite signals, does not transmit), serial NMEA output, provides latitude, longitude, altitude, speed, time, 3-5m accuracy, requires clear sky view, cold start 26s / warm start 1s, 9600 baud serial default, ceramic antenna or external antenna, used in vehicle tracking, drones, geo-logging projects

Typical Use Cases: Password entry, numeric input, menu navigation, calculator projects, door locks
Notes: 16 keys (0-9, A-D, *, #), 8 pins total (4 rows + 4 columns), matrix scanning to detect key press, debouncing handled by library, 3x4 version has 12 keys, membrane or mechanical versions available

Typical Use Cases: Basic image capture, computer vision, security cameras, object detection (limited)
Function Names: Requires complex configuration via I2C and parallel data capture
Notes: 640x480 VGA camera sensor, parallel data output (8-bit), I2C control interface (SCCB), very limited on standard Arduino (RAM constraints), works better with ESP32, requires external FIFO buffer (AL422) for Arduino, low frame rate on Arduino, used for basic image capture, requires significant processing power

Typical Use Cases: Inventory management, point-of-sale, product tracking, library systems
Notes: Laser or CCD barcode scanner, reads 1D (UPC, EAN, Code39) and/or 2D (QR, DataMatrix) codes, serial output (sends barcode as text), USB versions act as keyboard (no coding needed), trigger button or continuous scan mode, used in inventory systems, retail checkout, package tracking

Typical Use Cases: Access control, attendance systems, security locks, user authentication
Notes: Optical fingerprint sensor, 500 DPI resolution, stores up to 1000 fingerprints, serial communication (9600 baud), 3.3V or 5V versions, blue LED ring illumination, fingerprint enrollment and verification, matching accuracy >99%, used in door locks, safes, attendance systems

Typical Use Cases: Automotive diagnostics (OBD-II), vehicle communication, industrial control, robotics
Notes: Controller Area Network (CAN) transceiver, SPI interface to Arduino, supports CAN 2.0A/B protocols, 1 Mbps max speed, differential signaling, used in automotive (reading car sensors, engine data), industrial machinery, requires 120Ω termination resistors, TJA1050 or SN65HVD230 transceiver chip

Typical Use Cases: Current monitoring, power consumption tracking, battery monitoring, overcurrent protection
Notes: Hall effect current sensor, measures AC or DC current, analog voltage output (Vcc/2 at 0A), 5A/20A/30A versions, 185mV/A (5A), 100mV/A (20A), 66mV/A (30A) sensitivity, 5V power, bidirectional measurement

Typical Use Cases: Battery voltage monitoring, power supply monitoring, voltage detection, solar panel monitoring
Notes: Voltage divider module with 5:1 or 11:1 ratio, measures DC voltage up to 25V or 50V safely, analog output 0-5V proportional to input, simple resistor divider circuit, requires voltage calculation in code, LED indicator on some modules

Typical Use Cases: End-stop detection, CNC machines, 3D printers, position limiting, safety cutoffs
Notes: Mechanical switch activated by physical contact, SPDT configuration (COM, NO, NC), roller lever or straight lever actuator, very reliable, audible click, used extensively in motion control systems, typically rated 5A-16A

Typical Use Cases: Precise angle measurement, motor position feedback, robotic joints, dial/knob position
Function Names: getAngle(), getRawAngle(), getStatus(), setStartPosition(), begin(), readAngle(), readRawAngle()
Notes: 12-bit resolution (0-4095, 0.088° precision), contactless magnetic sensing, requires diametric magnet on shaft, I2C address 0x36, also has analog and PWM output modes, 360° continuous rotation measurement, no wear/tear (contactless)

Typical Use Cases: Precise rotation counting, motor speed measurement, position feedback, wheel odometry
Notes: Quadrature output (A, B phases), 360-600 pulses per revolution typical, direction detection via phase relationship, often includes index/Z pulse for reference, requires interrupt pins for best accuracy, industrial versions 5-24V, used in CNC, robotics

Typical Use Cases: Low-power displays, outdoor readability, digital signage, price tags, e-readers
Notes: Bistable display (retains image without power), extremely low power consumption, high contrast in sunlight, slow refresh (1-15 seconds), black/white or red/black/white versions, SPI interface, visible in direct sunlight, paper-like appearance, requires full refresh to avoid ghosting

Common versions: Micro SD Card Module, Full SD Card Module
Typical Use Cases: Data logging, file storage, audio/image storage, configuration files
Notes: SPI interface, reads/writes SD/SDHC cards (up to 32GB FAT32), requires 3.3V logic (has onboard voltage regulator for 5V Arduino), CS pin selectable, supports FAT16/FAT32 file systems, writes CSV/TXT/binary files, critical for data logging projects

Typical Use Cases: Configuration storage, calibration data, settings persistence, data retention without power
Notes: 32KB (AT24C256) external EEPROM, I2C interface, non-volatile storage, 1 million write cycles, I2C address 0x50-0x57 (configurable), retains data without power, slower than RAM but persistent, used when Arduino internal EEPROM insufficient

Typical Use Cases: Accurate timekeeping, data logging timestamps, alarm clocks, scheduled events, time-based automation
Notes: Battery-backed RTC, maintains time during power loss, CR2032 coin cell battery, I2C address 0x68, extremely accurate (±2 ppm, ~1 minute/year drift), built-in temperature compensated crystal oscillator (TCXO), 2 programmable alarms, includes temperature sensor, better accuracy than DS1307

Typical Use Cases: Voltage regulation, battery projects, power supply design, efficiency improvement
Notes: Step-down (buck) converter, input 4-40V, output 1.25-37V adjustable, 3A output current, ~92% efficiency, switching regulator (more efficient than linear), potentiometer for voltage adjustment, LED voltage display on some modules, heat sink included, used to power 5V Arduino from 12V battery

Typical Use Cases: Battery projects, LED drivers, voltage boosting, 12V from 5V USB
Notes: Step-up (boost) converter, input 2-24V, output 5-28V adjustable, 2A output current max, ~93% efficiency, switching regulator, potentiometer for voltage adjustment, can power 12V devices from 5V USB or single Li-ion cell, overcurrent protection, used to boost battery voltage

Typical Use Cases: Clean power supply, 3.3V for ESP modules, voltage regulation, noise-sensitive circuits
Notes: Low dropout linear regulator, fixed output (3.3V or 5V versions), 1A output current, input voltage: output + 1.3V minimum, less efficient than switching regulators (generates heat), very low noise output (good for analog/RF circuits), requires heat sink for high current, used to provide clean 3.3V for ESP8266/ESP32

Typical Use Cases: Li-ion/LiPo battery charging, portable projects, power banks, battery-powered devices
Notes: Charges single-cell Li-ion/LiPo (3.7V/4.2V), 1A charging current (adjustable via resistor), micro-USB input (5V), CC/CV charging profile, LED indicators (charging/done), protection version includes over-discharge, over-charge, over-current protection, auto-cutoff at 4.2V, used in all battery-powered Arduino projects with rechargeable batteries

Typical Use Cases: Magnetic locks, sorting systems, magnetic levitation, holding mechanisms
Notes: Coil-based magnet (only magnetic when powered), requires driver circuit (relay/MOSFET), typically 12V 0.5-2A, holding force 2.5kg-100kg depending on size, quick on/off response, used in door locks, electromagnetic games, sorting systems, requires flyback diode for coil protection

Typical Use Cases: Hydroponics, aquarium, water fountains, irrigation, cooling systems
Function Names: digitalWrite() (via relay/MOSFET), analogWrite() for speed control
Notes: Submersible DC pump, 3-12V operation, 100-240 L/hour flow rate, requires driver circuit for Arduino control, PWM for flow rate control, max lift height 40-200cm, low noise, used in water circulation, plant watering systems, aquarium filters

Typical Use Cases: Precise liquid dosing, chemical dispensing, medical applications, aquarium dosing
Function Names: digitalWrite() (via relay), stepper control if stepper motor version
Notes: Precise liquid pumping via squeezing tube with rollers, no contact between pump mechanism and liquid (sanitary), reversible flow, 12V DC motor or stepper motor driven, flow rate 0.5-100 ml/min typical, used in chemical dosing, aquarium supplements, 3D printing (paste extruders)

Typical Use Cases: Aquarium aeration, pneumatic actuators, air mattress inflation, pressure systems
Notes: Diaphragm-based air pump, 3-12V operation, ~1-3 L/min airflow, 0.1-0.3 bar pressure, requires driver circuit, relatively quiet operation, used for fish tank aerators, inflating bladders, pneumatic control, air cushions

Typical Use Cases: Electronics cooling, ventilation, heat dissipation, air circulation
Function Names: digitalWrite() (via MOSFET/relay), analogWrite() for speed control (4-pin PWM fans)
Notes: Standard PC cooling fan, 12V DC operation, 2-pin (power only) or 4-pin (PWM speed control + tachometer), airflow 10-100 CFM depending on size, requires driver circuit (MOSFET) for Arduino, PWM pin for speed control on 4-pin fans, used in electronics enclosures, 3D printer cooling

Typical Use Cases: Industrial communication, long-distance serial, Modbus RTU, multi-drop networks
Notes: Converts UART to differential RS485, half-duplex communication, up to 32 devices on single bus (multi-drop), 1200m cable distance at low baud rates, noise-resistant differential signaling, requires termination resistors (120Ω), used in industrial automation, building control systems, Modbus protocol

Pin Type: Digital I/O, Analog Input, PWM, SPI, I2C (bit-banged), USB (V-USB via firmware)
Typical Use Cases: Compact embedded control, USB mini devices, low-power sensor nodes
Notes: 8-bit AVR MCU, 8KB Flash, 512B SRAM, 6 GPIO pins

Common versions: ATmega328P-PU (DIP-28), ATmega328P-AU (TQFP), Arduino Uno bootloaded variant
Pin Type: Digital, Analog, PWM, UART, SPI, I2C, Interrupt, Power, Reset
Typical Use Cases: Embedded control, PWM generation, motor control, signal processing, Arduino-compatible systems
Function Names: pinMode(), digitalWrite(), analogRead(), analogWrite(), ISR(), sei(), cli()
Notes: 8-bit AVR MCU, 32KB Flash, 2KB SRAM, 1KB EEPROM. Core MCU used in Arduino Uno. Includes 3 timers, 6 PWM outputs, 10-bit ADC.

Typical Use Cases: Wireless data transmission, remote control systems, sensor data broadcasting, one-way RF communication, home automation transmitters
Notes: ASK (Amplitude Shift Keying) transmitter, operates at 433MHz, works with RadioHead RH_ASK library, data pin connects to any digital pin defined in RH_ASK constructor, typical range 20-200m (depends on antenna and voltage), 3.3V-12V operation (higher voltage = longer range), simple wire antenna improves range significantly (17.3cm for 433MHz quarter-wave), one-way TX only — pair with receiver module on second Arduino, transmission speed up to 2000 bps with RH_ASK

Typical Use Cases: Wireless data reception, remote control systems, sensor data receiving, one-way RF communication, home automation receivers
Notes: ASK (Amplitude Shift Keying) receiver, operates at 433MHz, works with RadioHead RH_ASK library, data pin connects to any digital pin defined in RH_ASK constructor (typically has 2 DATA pins — use either), 5V operation recommended for best sensitivity, noisy output when no signal present (normal behavior), simple wire antenna significantly improves reception range (17.3cm quarter-wave), must be paired with matching 433MHz ASK transmitter on sender Arduino, SPI.h must be included even though SPI is not actively used (RadioHead dependency), requires two Arduinos for full TX/RX demo

Typical Use Cases: SPI/I2C/UART communication projects, low-cost embedded control, interfacing with Arduino via SPI, industrial automation, Nuvoton ecosystem projects
Notes: Nuvoton 8051-core 1T MCU, 18KB Flash, 1KB SRAM, 20-pin package, 3.3V logic (NOT 5V tolerant — use level shifter with Arduino Uno/Nano), SPI/I2C/UART peripherals, programmed via Nuvoton SDK and ICP Tool (not Arduino IDE), requires separate programmer (Nu-Link), commonly used as SPI slave paired with Arduino master, 16 MHz clock, very low cost
